growth_records.medium: MM63 minimal medium + 0.17 M NaCl, glucose
growth_records.cite: Hobmeier, Karina et al. Anaplerotic Pathways in Halomonas elongata: The Role of the Sodium Gradient. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.561800. [PMC7545133]
growth_records.snippet: experienced difficulties when growing on glucose (0.06 ± 0.04 h−1 vs. 0.28 ± 0.007 h−1)
growth_records.notes: Wild type on glucose at low salt (0.17 M NaCl). Reported max growth rate mu=0.28 ± 0.007 h-1, obtained by linear regression of log(OD600) over sliding windows (=calculated from OD slope). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.28=2.48 h. No experimental uptake/secretion rates reported; substrate/product exchange fluxes in the paper are TMFA model predictions (not measured), so omitted. Ectoine content was measured (Fig 9, mmol/gDW vs biomass) but no explicit numeric value given in text; not a secretion rate (intracellular pool). The 4.5 mmol/gDW value in the paper is a modeling ATP-production constraint, not experimental.

growth_records.medium: MM63 minimal medium + 0.17 M NaCl, acetate
growth_records.cite: Hobmeier, Karina et al. Anaplerotic Pathways in Halomonas elongata: The Role of the Sodium Gradient. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.561800. [PMC7545133]
growth_records.snippet: similar growth rates as the wild type when growing on acetate (0.16 ± 0.02 h−1 vs. 0.19 ± 0.004 h−1)
growth_records.notes: Wild type on acetate at low salt (0.17 M NaCl). mu=0.19 ± 0.004 h-1 from log(OD) slope (calculated). td=ln2/0.19=3.65 h. No measured uptake/secretion rates (model-predicted only).

growth_records.medium: MM63 minimal medium + 0.17 M NaCl, glucose
growth_records.cite: Hobmeier, Karina et al. Anaplerotic Pathways in Halomonas elongata: The Role of the Sodium Gradient. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.561800. [PMC7545133]
growth_records.snippet: experienced difficulties when growing on glucose (0.06 ± 0.04 h−1 vs. 0.28 ± 0.007 h−1)
growth_records.notes: Ppc-deletion mutant on glucose at low salt (0.17 M NaCl). mu=0.06 ± 0.04 h-1 (calculated from OD slope); note large relative error. td=ln2/0.06=11.55 h. Engineered knockout strain, not wild type.

growth_records.medium: MM63 minimal medium + 0.17 M NaCl, acetate
growth_records.cite: Hobmeier, Karina et al. Anaplerotic Pathways in Halomonas elongata: The Role of the Sodium Gradient. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.561800. [PMC7545133]
growth_records.snippet: similar growth rates as the wild type when growing on acetate (0.16 ± 0.02 h−1 vs. 0.19 ± 0.004 h−1)
growth_records.notes: Ppc-deletion mutant on acetate at low salt (0.17 M NaCl). mu=0.16 ± 0.02 h-1 (calculated from OD slope). td=ln2/0.16=4.33 h. Grows near wild-type rate on acetate (Ppc not needed for anaplerosis on acetate).

growth_records.medium: MM63 minimal medium + 2 M NaCl, glucose
growth_records.cite: Hobmeier, Karina et al. Anaplerotic Pathways in Halomonas elongata: The Role of the Sodium Gradient. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.561800. [PMC7545133]
growth_records.snippet: the dramatic drop from a growth rate of 0.25 ± 0.01 h−1 in the wild type to 0.09 ± 0.004 h−1 in H. elongata-OAD
growth_records.notes: Wild type on glucose at high salt (2 M NaCl). mu=0.25 ± 0.01 h-1 (calculated from OD slope; growth rates from shake-flask experiments per Fig 10). td=ln2/0.25=2.77 h.

growth_records.medium: MM63 minimal medium + 2 M NaCl, glucose
growth_records.cite: Hobmeier, Karina et al. Anaplerotic Pathways in Halomonas elongata: The Role of the Sodium Gradient. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.561800. [PMC7545133]
growth_records.snippet: to 0.09 ± 0.004 h−1 in H. elongata-OAD ... This strain grew roughly 30% slower (μ = 0.09
growth_records.notes: Oad-deletion mutant on glucose at high salt (2 M NaCl). mu=0.09 ± 0.004 h-1 (calculated). td=ln2/0.09=7.70 h. Oad important for growth at high salt. Engineered knockout.

growth_records.medium: MM63 minimal medium + 2 M NaCl, glucose
growth_records.cite: Hobmeier, Karina et al. Anaplerotic Pathways in Halomonas elongata: The Role of the Sodium Gradient. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2020.561800. [PMC7545133]
growth_records.snippet: a smaller decrease to a growth rate of 0.13 ± 0.01 h−1 is shown in the Ppc lacking strain
growth_records.notes: Ppc-deletion mutant on glucose at high salt (2 M NaCl). mu=0.13 ± 0.01 h-1 (calculated). td=ln2/0.13=5.33 h. Ppc less essential at high salt (steep Na+ gradient lets Oad supply OAA). Engineered knockout.

growth_records.medium: HE9 synthetic medium (6% NaCl), glucose-limited chemostat

mfa.note: MFA-calculated at mu=0.10; measured Yco2/S=2.41±0.09, error 0.5%
relative net flux at mu=0.10, 6% NaCl

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Decoding metabolic trade-offs in Halomonas elongata: Chemostat-based flux remodeling for industrial ectoine biosynthesis. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2025.07.004. [PMC12318276]
growth_records.method: reported (D=mu at chemostat steady state); td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.103=6.73 h
growth_records.snippet: qo2 and qco2, as well as qp and qs, increased with D, reaching 6.80 and 6.74 mmol/g DCW/h, 372.05 umol/g DCW/h, and 3.04 mmol/g DCW/h at D=0.35 h-1 ... Table 1: D=0.103, qs 1.12, qo2 2.61, qco2 2.70, qp 71.12, Yx/s 0.51
growth_records.notes: Direct chemostat steady-state values from Table 1, 6% NaCl, D=0.103 h-1 (mu=D). qs (glucose uptake)=1.12 mmol/gDCW/h; qo2=2.61; qco2=2.70; qp(ectoine)=71.12 umol/gDCW/h. td=ln2/mu=6.73 h. Ectoine is intracellular (not secreted). Temperature: seed stated 37C; fermenter T not explicitly restated in text so assumed same as seed (37C). MFA relative fluxes from Fig 3 text for mu=0.10.

growth_records.medium: HE9 synthetic medium (13% NaCl), glucose-limited chemostat

up.method: Herbert-Pirt fit qs=mu/YG+m=0.10/0.897+0.056=0.1675 g/gDCW/h /180.16*1000=0.93 mmol/gDCW/h (YG,m from Table 2)

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Decoding metabolic trade-offs in Halomonas elongata: Chemostat-based flux remodeling for industrial ectoine biosynthesis. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2025.07.004. [PMC12318276]
growth_records.snippet: For the 13% salt... Cx and Cp reaching maxima of 15.89 and 3.03 g/L at D=0.30, 1.29 and 1.67x higher than at D=0.10... Yp/s 0.11 and pectoine 1.03 mmol/g at D=0.10... qco2 decreased by 10.4% (mu=0.10) under 13% vs 6%
growth_records.notes: 13% NaCl, D=mu=0.10 h-1. Per-condition qs/qo2/qco2/qp values are in Fig S5/Table S2 (not numeric in main text); values here computed: qs and qo2 from Herbert-Pirt/Monod fits (Table 2: YG=0.897, m=0.056; Yx/o2=143.472 g/mol, mo2=1.488) => model-fit (is_experimental=false). qco2 derived from stated 10.4% reduction vs measured 6% value 2.70 => 2.42 (experimental basis). qp from fitted qp=1.504mu-0.0633. Cx=15.89/1.29=12.32; Cp=3.03/1.67=1.81; Yco2/S=1.83/(1-0.304)=2.63. Yp/s (0.11) and pectoine (1.03) stated directly. td=6.93 h.

growth_records.medium: HE9 synthetic medium (13% NaCl), glucose-limited chemostat
growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Decoding metabolic trade-offs in Halomonas elongata: Chemostat-based flux remodeling for industrial ectoine biosynthesis. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2025.07.004. [PMC12318276]
growth_records.snippet: 13% salt: Yp/s 0.11 and 0.13 mmol/mmol, pectoine 1.03 and 1.05 mmol/g at D=0.10 and 0.20... qco2 decreased by 46.7% at mu=0.20 under 13% vs 6%; Yco2/S 41.5% lower at mu=0.20
growth_records.notes: 13% NaCl, D=mu=0.20 h-1. qs/qo2 are Herbert-Pirt/Monod model fits (Table 2 params) => is_experimental false. qco2=5.17*(1-0.467)=2.76 from stated 46.7% reduction of measured 6% qco2. qp from fitted eqn. Yp/s (0.13) and pectoine (1.05) stated directly. Per-D biomass/Cs at 13% mu=0.20 only in Fig S5/Table S2. td=3.47 h.

growth_records.medium: HE9 synthetic medium (13% NaCl), glucose-limited chemostat

up.method: Herbert-Pirt fit qs=0.30/0.897+0.056=0.3905 g/gDCW/h /180.16*1000=2.17 mmol/gDCW/h (Table 2); note qs lower than 6% (2.59) as stated

sec.method: qp=D*Cp/Cx=0.30*3.03/15.89=0.0572 g/gDCW/h /142.15*1000=402 umol/gDCW/h; fitted eqn gives 388

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Decoding metabolic trade-offs in Halomonas elongata: Chemostat-based flux remodeling for industrial ectoine biosynthesis. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2025.07.004. [PMC12318276]
growth_records.snippet: 13% salt, D=0.30: Cx 15.89 g/L, Cp 3.03 g/L, Yp/s 0.20 mmol/mmol, pectoine 1.34 mmol/g, Yco2/s 1.83 mmol/mmol. ED proportion 61%; NADPH 94.4, NADH 352.9 mmol/100mmol glucose
growth_records.notes: 13% NaCl, D=mu=0.30 h-1 - highest-yield condition, directly stated: Cx=15.89, Cp=3.03 g/L, Yp/s=0.20 mmol/mmol, pectoine=1.34 mmol/g, Yco2/S=1.83. qp computed as D*Cp/Cx=0.30*3.03/15.89/142.15*1000=402 umol/gDCW/h (experimental basis; fitted eqn 388). qco2=6.41*(1-0.412)=3.77 from stated 41.2% reduction. qs/qo2 Herbert-Pirt/Monod fits (Table 2). MFA % changes vs 6% and NADPH/NADH production fluxes from Fig 4 text. td=2.31 h.

growth_records.medium: Glucose-based fermentation medium (composition per ref 19; initial glucose 21.65 g/L, initial NaCl 1% w/v; contains organic nitrogen source)

up.method: specific oxygen uptake rate qO2 (off-gas), Table 1
volumetric oxygen uptake rate OUR (off-gas), Table 1

sec.method: specific CO2 evolution rate qCO2 (off-gas), Table 1
volumetric CO2 evolution rate CER (off-gas), Table 1
specific ectoine production rate qp, Table 1

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Temporal dynamics of stress response in Halomonas elongata to NaCl shock: physiological, metabolomic, and transcriptomic insights. Microbial Cell Factories (2024). DOI:10.1186/s12934-024-02358-5. [PMC10960403]
growth_records.method: reported (mu in Table 1; doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.18=3.85 h)
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 Before shock: OUR 127.83±5.43 mmol/L/h, qO2 6.53±0.18 mmol/g/h, CER 130.15±6.23, qCO2 6.65±0.21, Biomass 19.58±0.23 g/L, mu 0.18±0.02 h-1, Glucose 21.65±0.65 g/L, qp 1.35±0.01 mg ectoine/gDCW/h
growth_records.notes: Pre-shock baseline (late-exponential batch, 1% NaCl). All rates experimental off-gas/analytical measurements from Table 1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.18=3.85 h. Batch 5 L bioreactor, 3 L WV, 37C, 1.2 vvm, pH 7.0 (ammonia). Medium composition not stated here (cited to ref 19); glucose 21.65 g/L, NaCl 1% given. Biomass 19.58 g/L. Ectoine is intracellular.

growth_records.medium: Glucose-based fermentation medium (composition per ref 19; initial glucose 21.65 g/L, NaCl 1% w/v)

up.method: derived qS from measured residual glucose over 4 h with mean biomass

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Temporal dynamics of stress response in Halomonas elongata to NaCl shock: physiological, metabolomic, and transcriptomic insights. Microbial Cell Factories (2024). DOI:10.1186/s12934-024-02358-5. [PMC10960403]
growth_records.notes: Unshocked 1% NaCl control at 4 h. qO2/OUR/qCO2/CER/mu/qp experimental (Table 1). Derived glucose qS: consumed=21.65-0.66=20.99 g/L over 4 h; mean biomass=(19.58+23.38)/2=21.48 g/L; qS=20.99/(4*21.48)=0.2443 g/gDCW/h; /180.16*1000=1.36 mmol/gDCW/h (is_experimental:false, approximate). Ectoine titre 1.75 g/L. Late-batch slowdown from glucose near-depletion (0.66 g/L).

growth_records.medium: Glucose-based fermentation medium + NaCl shock to 5% (w/v)

up.method: derived qS from measured residual glucose over 4 h with mean biomass

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Temporal dynamics of stress response in Halomonas elongata to NaCl shock: physiological, metabolomic, and transcriptomic insights. Microbial Cell Factories (2024). DOI:10.1186/s12934-024-02358-5. [PMC10960403]
growth_records.notes: 5% NaCl osmotic shock, 4 h. Experimental qO2/OUR/qCO2/CER/mu/qp (Table 1). Derived glucose qS: consumed=21.65-1.82=19.83 g/L over 4 h; mean biomass=(19.58+31.41)/2=25.495 g/L; qS=19.83/(4*25.495)=0.1945 g/gDCW/h=1.08 mmol/gDCW/h (approx). Biomass rose to 31.41 g/L; ectoine 4.08 g/L; max ectoine volumetric productivity 1230 mg/L/h.

growth_records.medium: Glucose-based fermentation medium + NaCl shock to 8% (w/v)

up.method: derived qS from measured residual glucose over 4 h with mean biomass

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Temporal dynamics of stress response in Halomonas elongata to NaCl shock: physiological, metabolomic, and transcriptomic insights. Microbial Cell Factories (2024). DOI:10.1186/s12934-024-02358-5. [PMC10960403]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 8% NaCl (4 h): OUR 77.91±9.54, qO2 3.29±0.11, CER 82.42±6.87, qCO2 3.48±0.15, Biomass 23.65±0.78 g/L, mu 0.05±0.01 h-1, Glucose 8.71±0.61 g/L, qp 66.54±1.86 mg ectoine/gDCW/h, ectoine P 4.58±0.19 g/L, max productivity 1450±99 mg/L/h
growth_records.notes: 8% NaCl shock, 4 h. Experimental qO2/OUR/qCO2/CER/mu/qp (Table 1). qp 66.54 mg/gDCW/h = 5.5-fold control. Derived glucose qS: consumed=21.65-8.71=12.94 g/L over 4 h; mean biomass=(19.58+23.65)/2=21.615 g/L; qS=12.94/(4*21.615)=0.1497 g/gDCW/h=0.83 mmol/gDCW/h (approx). Max ectoine productivity 1450 mg/L/h; ectoine 4.58 g/L.

growth_records.medium: Glucose-based fermentation medium + NaCl shock to 13% (w/v)

up.method: derived qS from measured residual glucose over 4 h with mean biomass

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Temporal dynamics of stress response in Halomonas elongata to NaCl shock: physiological, metabolomic, and transcriptomic insights. Microbial Cell Factories (2024). DOI:10.1186/s12934-024-02358-5. [PMC10960403]
growth_records.notes: 13% NaCl shock (above tolerance); strong inhibition of growth and respiration (OUR down ~90%). Experimental qO2/OUR/qCO2/CER/mu (Table 1); ectoine biosynthesis arrested (qp 0, net productivity slightly negative). Derived glucose qS: consumed=21.65-19.12=2.53 g/L over 4 h; mean biomass=(19.58+17.38)/2=18.48 g/L; qS=2.53/(4*18.48)=0.0342 g/gDCW/h=0.19 mmol/gDCW/h (approx).

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 6011 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/6011

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 6011 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/6011
